# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Performance and engine fixes**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 delivers targeted performance wins and upstream Skia m151 bug fixes. Hot paths in tag parsing and glyph shaping have been optimized with zero-copy spans and allocation-free overloads, and two rounds of upstream Skia m151 engine fixes have been merged.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## Engine

- **Skia m151 upstream bug fixes merged** — Two rounds of upstream Chrome m151 engine fixes have been pulled in, keeping the bundled renderer current with the latest Skia servicing patches. ([#4312](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4312), [#4382](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4382))

## API Surface

- **SKColor hex parsing optimized with ReadOnlySpan overloads** — SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char> directly, eliminating string allocations in hot color-parsing paths. ([#4345](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4345))
- **GRContextOptions unsafe modifier removed** — The unnecessary unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions has been removed, making the type usable in projects that do not allow unsafe code. ([#3991](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3991))

## Bug Fixes

- **SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans** — Reading shaping glyph results now uses Span-based access instead of element-by-element P/Invoke calls, eliminating per-glyph interop overhead. ([#4388](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4388))
- **Per-call allocations removed from tag parsing** — SKFourByteTag.Parse and HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse no longer allocate a char[4] on every call, reducing GC pressure in layout-heavy workloads. ([#4396](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4396), [#4408](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4408))
